topics.title: Certificate Revocation
topics.definition: Certificate Revocation is the process of declaring a certificate invalid before its expiration date. The CA publishes the revocation so that clients stop trusting the certificate.
examPoints: Certificate Revocation is the process of declaring a certificate invalid before its expiration date. The CA publishes the revocation so that clients stop trusting the certificate.
topics.narration:
Certificate Revocation

Certificate Revocation is the process of declaring a certificate invalid before its expiration date. The CA publishes the revocation so that clients stop trusting the certificate.

Important exam points. Certificate Revocation is the process of declaring a certificate invalid before its expiration date. The CA publishes the revocation so that clients stop trusting the certificate.. Revoked certificate = Untrusted. Revocation occurs before expiration

topics.title: Why Revoke a Certificate?
topics.definition: Certificates are revoked when they can no longer be trusted due to security, operational, or policy reasons.
examPoints: Certificates are revoked when they can no longer be trusted due to security, operational, or policy reasons.
topics.narration:
Why Revoke a Certificate?

Certificates are revoked when they can no longer be trusted due to security, operational, or policy reasons.

Important exam points. Certificates are revoked when they can no longer be trusted due to security, operational, or policy reasons.. Common Reasons:. Private Key Compromise. Wrong Certificate Issued. Employee Left. Device Lost. CA Compromise

topics.title: Security Impact of Revocation
topics.definition: Revocation prevents attackers from continuing to misuse compromised or invalid certificates.
examPoints: Revocation prevents attackers from continuing to misuse compromised or invalid certificates.
Attackers can impersonate legitimate users or servers.
topics.narration:
Security Impact of Revocation

Revocation prevents attackers from continuing to misuse compromised or invalid certificates.

Important exam points. Revocation prevents attackers from continuing to misuse compromised or invalid certificates.. Without revocation:. Stolen certificates remain trusted.. Attackers can impersonate legitimate users or servers.

topics.title: Enterprise Revocation Policy
topics.definition: Organizations define policies specifying who can request revocation, when it should occur, and how revocation information is published.
examPoints: Organizations define policies specifying who can request revocation, when it should occur, and how revocation information is published.
topics.narration:
Enterprise Revocation Policy

Organizations define policies specifying who can request revocation, when it should occur, and how revocation information is published.

Important exam points. Organizations define policies specifying who can request revocation, when it should occur, and how revocation information is published.. Policy includes:. Approval Process. Response Time. Revocation Method

topics.title: Certificate Revocation Workflow
workflows: Compromise Detected ↓ Request Revocation ↓ CA Verifies Request ↓ Certificate Marked Revoked ↓ CRL Updated / OCSP Updated ↓ Clients Reject Certificate
topics.narration:
Certificate Revocation Workflow

Workflow. Compromise Detected ↓ Request Revocation ↓ CA Verifies Request ↓ Certificate Marked Revoked ↓ CRL Updated / OCSP Updated ↓ Clients Reject Certificate

topics.title: Certificate Revocation List (CRL)
topics.definition: A CRL (Certificate Revocation List) is a digitally signed list of revoked certificate serial numbers published by the CA.
examPoints: A CRL (Certificate Revocation List) is a digitally signed list of revoked certificate serial numbers published by the CA.
topics.narration:
Certificate Revocation List (CRL)

A CRL (Certificate Revocation List) is a digitally signed list of revoked certificate serial numbers published by the CA.

Important exam points. A CRL (Certificate Revocation List) is a digitally signed list of revoked certificate serial numbers published by the CA.. Contains:. Revoked Serial Numbers. Revocation Date. Revocation Reason

topics.title: CRL Generation
topics.definition: The CA generates a CRL from its certificate database after revoking certificates.
examPoints: The CA generates a CRL from its certificate database after revoking certificates.
topics.narration:
CRL Generation

The CA generates a CRL from its certificate database after revoking certificates.

Important exam points. The CA generates a CRL from its certificate database after revoking certificates.. Signed using. CA Private Key

topics.title: CRL Structure
topics.definition: A CRL contains information required for clients to verify revocation status.
examPoints: A CRL contains information required for clients to verify revocation status.
topics.narration:
CRL Structure

A CRL contains information required for clients to verify revocation status.

Important exam points. A CRL contains information required for clients to verify revocation status.. Issuer. This Update. Next Update. Revoked Certificates. Signature

topics.title: CRL Distribution Point (CDP)
topics.definition: The CRL Distribution Point specifies where clients can download the CRL.
examPoints: The CRL Distribution Point specifies where clients can download the CRL.
topics.narration:
CRL Distribution Point (CDP)

The CRL Distribution Point specifies where clients can download the CRL.

Important exam points. The CRL Distribution Point specifies where clients can download the CRL.. Common Locations. HTTP. LDAP

topics.title: CRL Validation
topics.definition: Clients download the CRL, verify its signature, and search for the certificate's serial number.
examPoints: Clients download the CRL, verify its signature, and search for the certificate's serial number.
topics.narration:
CRL Validation

Clients download the CRL, verify its signature, and search for the certificate's serial number.

Important exam points. Clients download the CRL, verify its signature, and search for the certificate's serial number.. If serial number exists. ↓. Certificate is Revoked

topics.title: CRL Advantages
topics.narration:
CRL Advantages

Important exam points. Widely Supported. Easy Implementation

topics.title: CRL Limitations
topics.narration:
CRL Limitations

Important exam points. Periodic Updates. Can Become Outdated

topics.title: Delta CRL
topics.definition: A Delta CRL contains only the changes since the last Full CRL.
examPoints: A Delta CRL contains only the changes since the last Full CRL.
topics.narration:
Delta CRL

A Delta CRL contains only the changes since the last Full CRL.

Important exam points. A Delta CRL contains only the changes since the last Full CRL.. Smaller. Faster

topics.title: Why Delta CRL?
topics.definition: Reduces bandwidth and download time for large PKIs.
examPoints: Reduces bandwidth and download time for large PKIs.
topics.narration:
Why Delta CRL?

Reduces bandwidth and download time for large PKIs.

Important exam points. Reduces bandwidth and download time for large PKIs.. Only recent revocations. Not complete list

topics.title: Full CRL vs Delta CRL
comparisons: Full CRL: Complete List; Delta CRL: Only Changes
Full CRL: Larger; Delta CRL: Smaller
Full CRL: Less Frequent; Delta CRL: More Frequent
topics.narration:
Full CRL vs Delta CRL

Comparison. Full CRL: Complete List; Delta CRL: Only Changes. Full CRL: Larger; Delta CRL: Smaller. Full CRL: Less Frequent; Delta CRL: More Frequent

topics.title: Delta CRL Synchronization
topics.definition: Clients combine the Base CRL with the Delta CRL to obtain the latest revocation information.
examPoints: Clients combine the Base CRL with the Delta CRL to obtain the latest revocation information.
topics.narration:
Delta CRL Synchronization

Clients combine the Base CRL with the Delta CRL to obtain the latest revocation information.

Important exam points. Clients combine the Base CRL with the Delta CRL to obtain the latest revocation information.. Base CRL

topics.title: Online Certificate Status Protocol (OCSP)
topics.definition: OCSP provides real-time certificate status without downloading the entire CRL.
examPoints: OCSP provides real-time certificate status without downloading the entire CRL.
topics.narration:
Online Certificate Status Protocol (OCSP)

OCSP provides real-time certificate status without downloading the entire CRL.

Important exam points. OCSP provides real-time certificate status without downloading the entire CRL.. Good. Revoked. Unknown

topics.title: OCSP Workflow
workflows: Client ↓ OCSP Request ↓ OCSP Responder ↓ Signed Response ↓ Certificate Status
topics.narration:
OCSP Workflow

Workflow. Client ↓ OCSP Request ↓ OCSP Responder ↓ Signed Response ↓ Certificate Status

topics.title: OCSP Responder
topics.definition: A server that answers certificate status requests.
examPoints: A server that answers certificate status requests.
topics.narration:
OCSP Responder

A server that answers certificate status requests.

Important exam points. A server that answers certificate status requests.. Usually operated by. CA

topics.title: OCSP vs CRL
comparisons: CRL: Download Entire List; OCSP: Query One Certificate
CRL: Larger; OCSP: Smaller
CRL: Periodic; OCSP: Real-Time
topics.narration:
OCSP vs CRL

Comparison. CRL: Download Entire List; OCSP: Query One Certificate. CRL: Larger; OCSP: Smaller. CRL: Periodic; OCSP: Real-Time

topics.title: OCSP Advantages
topics.narration:
OCSP Advantages

Important exam points. Small Response. Near Real-Time

topics.title: OCSP Limitations
topics.narration:
OCSP Limitations

Important exam points. Depends on OCSP Server Availability

topics.title: OCSP Stapling
topics.definition: The web server retrieves the OCSP response and sends it to the client during the TLS handshake.
examPoints: The web server retrieves the OCSP response and sends it to the client during the TLS handshake.
topics.narration:
OCSP Stapling

The web server retrieves the OCSP response and sends it to the client during the TLS handshake.

Important exam points. The web server retrieves the OCSP response and sends it to the client during the TLS handshake.. Server performs. OCSP Lookup

topics.title: Why OCSP Stapling?
topics.narration:
Why OCSP Stapling?

Important exam points. Speed. Privacy. Performance

topics.title: OCSP Stapling Workflow
workflows: Server ↓ Gets OCSP Response ↓ Caches Response ↓ Sends During TLS Handshake ↓ Client Verifies
topics.narration:
OCSP Stapling Workflow

Workflow. Server ↓ Gets OCSP Response ↓ Caches Response ↓ Sends During TLS Handshake ↓ Client Verifies

topics.title: Browser Support
topics.narration:
Browser Support

Important exam points. OCSP Stapling

topics.title: Revocation Reasons
topics.definition: The CA records the reason why a certificate was revoked.
examPoints: The CA records the reason why a certificate was revoked.
topics.narration:
Revocation Reasons

The CA records the reason why a certificate was revoked.

Important exam points. The CA records the reason why a certificate was revoked.. Key Compromise. CA Compromise. Affiliation Changed. Superseded. Privilege Withdrawn

topics.title: Key Compromise
topics.definition: The certificate's private key has been exposed or stolen.
examPoints: The certificate's private key has been exposed or stolen.
topics.narration:
Key Compromise

The certificate's private key has been exposed or stolen.

Important exam points. The certificate's private key has been exposed or stolen.. Most Serious. Generate New Key Pair

topics.title: CA Compromise
topics.definition: The CA's private key is compromised.
examPoints: The CA's private key is compromised.
topics.narration:
CA Compromise

The CA's private key is compromised.

Important exam points. The CA's private key is compromised.. Extremely Critical. May require rebuilding PKI.

topics.title: Affiliation Changed
topics.definition: The certificate owner's organizational relationship has changed.
examPoints: The certificate owner's organizational relationship has changed.
topics.narration:
Affiliation Changed

The certificate owner's organizational relationship has changed.

Important exam points. The certificate owner's organizational relationship has changed.. Employee Leaves Company

topics.title: Superseded
topics.definition: The certificate has been replaced with a newer certificate.
examPoints: The certificate has been replaced with a newer certificate.
topics.narration:
Superseded

The certificate has been replaced with a newer certificate.

Important exam points. The certificate has been replaced with a newer certificate.. Usually after. Renewal. Rekey

topics.title: Cessation of Operation
topics.definition: The service or organization no longer operates.
examPoints: The service or organization no longer operates.
topics.narration:
Cessation of Operation

The service or organization no longer operates.

Important exam points. The service or organization no longer operates.. Website Shutdown

topics.title: Privilege Withdrawn
topics.definition: The certificate owner no longer has permission to perform the associated activity.
examPoints: The certificate owner no longer has permission to perform the associated activity.
topics.narration:
Privilege Withdrawn

The certificate owner no longer has permission to perform the associated activity.

Important exam points. The certificate owner no longer has permission to perform the associated activity.. Admin Rights Removed

topics.title: Certificate Hold
topics.definition: Temporary suspension of a certificate.
topics.narration:
Certificate Hold

Temporary suspension of a certificate.

Important exam points. Temporary suspension of a certificate.. Can later be restored.

topics.title: Remove From CRL
topics.definition: Removes a certificate from the temporary Hold state.
examPoints: Removes a certificate from the temporary Hold state.
topics.narration:
Remove From CRL

Removes a certificate from the temporary Hold state.

Important exam points. Removes a certificate from the temporary Hold state.. Applies only to. Certificate Hold

topics.title: Expired Certificate
topics.definition: A certificate automatically becomes invalid after the Not After date.
examPoints: A certificate automatically becomes invalid after the Not After date.
topics.narration:
Expired Certificate

A certificate automatically becomes invalid after the Not After date.

Important exam points. A certificate automatically becomes invalid after the Not After date.. Automatic. Time Based

comparisons: Expired: Automatic; Revoked: Manual
Expired: Time-Based; Revoked: Security-Based
Expired: Planned; Revoked: Emergency

topics.title: Certificate Compromise
topics.definition: Occurs when a certificate or its private key can no longer be trusted.
examPoints: Occurs when a certificate or its private key can no longer be trusted.

Certificate Compromise

Occurs when a certificate or its private key can no longer be trusted.

Important exam points. Occurs when a certificate or its private key can no longer be trusted.. Stolen Private Key. Leaked PFX. Hacked Server

topics.narration:

workflows: Detect ↓ Contain ↓ Revoke ↓ Generate New Key ↓ Create CSR ↓ Issue Certificate ↓ Install ↓ Monitor

Incident Response

Workflow. Detect ↓ Contain ↓ Revoke ↓ Generate New Key ↓ Create CSR ↓ Issue Certificate ↓ Install ↓ Monitor

examPoints: After compromise, a completely new key pair must be generated.
topics.narration:
Rekey after Compromise

After compromise, a completely new key pair must be generated.

Important exam points. After compromise, a completely new key pair must be generated.. Never reuse. Compromised Key

Recovery Process

Important exam points. Generate Keys. New CSR. New Certificate. Deploy. Remove Old Keys

topics.narration:

topics.title: OpenSSL Commands
topics.narration:
OpenSSL Commands

Commands. Generate CRL: openssl ca -gencrl -out ca.crl

topics.title: Phase 7 - Certificate Revocation
topics.narration:
Phase 7 - Certificate Revocation

Commands. View CRL: openssl crl -in ca.crl -text -noout

topics.title: Phase 7 - Certificate Revocation
topics.narration:
Phase 7 - Certificate Revocation

Commands. Revoke Certificate: openssl ca -revoke server.crt

topics.title: Phase 7 - Certificate Revocation
commands: Verify Certificate with CRL: openssl verify \ -CAfile root.crt \ -CRLfile ca.crl \ -crl_check server.crt
topics.narration:
Phase 7 - Certificate Revocation

Commands. Verify Certificate with CRL: openssl verify \ -CAfile root.crt \ -CRLfile ca.crl \ -crl_check server.crt

topics.title: Phase 7 - Certificate Revocation
commands: Check OCSP: openssl ocsp \ -issuer subCA.crt \ -cert server.crt \ -url http://ocsp.example.com
topics.narration:
Phase 7 - Certificate Revocation

Commands. Check OCSP: openssl ocsp \ -issuer subCA.crt \ -cert server.crt \ -url http://ocsp.example.com

topics.title: Top 35 One-Liners for Exam
oneLiners: Revocation invalidates a certificate before expiration.
Expiration happens automatically at Not After.
CRL = Certificate Revocation List.
CRL contains revoked serial numbers.
CRLs are digitally signed by the CA.
Clients verify CRL signatures before trusting them.
CRL Distribution Point tells clients where to download the CRL.
Delta CRL contains only recent changes.
Delta CRL requires a Base CRL.
OCSP provides real-time certificate status.
OCSP status values: Good, Revoked, Unknown.
OCSP queries one certificate at a time.
OCSP responses are signed.
OCSP Stapling lets the server send the cached OCSP response.
OCSP Stapling improves performance and privacy.
Browsers prefer OCSP Stapling when available.
The most common revocation reason is Key Compromise.
CA Compromise is the most severe PKI incident.
Affiliation Changed means the owner's relationship changed.
Superseded means a newer certificate replaced the old one.
Certificate Hold is a temporary suspension.
Remove from CRL releases a certificate from Hold status.
Expired certificates are no longer valid even if never revoked.
Revoked certificates must not be trusted even if still within their validity period.
A TLS handshake should fail for revoked certificates.
Private-key compromise requires rekeying.
Never reuse a compromised private key.
Incident response begins with detection.
Recovery includes revocation, rekeying, and reissuance.
HSMs help protect CA private keys.
Monitor certificate expiration proactively.
Automate renewal where possible.
Use both CRL and OCSP where appropriate.
Certificate Transparency logs can help detect unauthorized certificate issuance.
The most important workflow to memorize is: Detect → Revoke → CRL/OCSP Update → New Key → New CSR → New Certificate → Deploy → Destroy Old Key.

OCSP Stapling lets the server send the cached OCSP response.. OCSP Stapling improves performance and privacy.. Browsers prefer OCSP Stapling when available.. Superseded means a newer certificate replaced the old one.. Certificate Hold is a temporary suspension.. Remove from CRL releases a certificate from Hold status.. Expired certificates are no longer valid even if never revoked.. Revoked certificates must not be trusted even if still within their validity period.. A TLS handshake should fail for revoked certificates.. Private-key compromise requires rekeying.. Never reuse a compromised private key.. Incident response begins with detection.. Recovery includes revocation, rekeying, and reissuance.. HSMs help protect CA private keys.. Monitor certificate expiration proactively.. Automate renewal where possible.. Use both CRL and OCSP where appropriate.. Certificate Transparency logs can help detect unauthorized certificate issuance.. The most important workflow to memorize is: Detect → Revoke → CRL/OCSP Update → New Key → New CSR → New Certificate → Deploy → Destroy Old Key.
